# Synthetic Test Database Report

## Overview

A document management system schema (`schema.sql`) with 8 tables, designed to cover PostgreSQL features missing from the existing test databases (Pagila, Chinook, Northwind).

**Tables:** users, categories, documents, document_permissions, tags, document_tags, audit_log, booking_slots

## Feature Coverage Checklist

Legend: **NEW** = not covered by other test DBs, **EXISTING** = already covered elsewhere

### Types & Domains

- [x] **NEW** — `CREATE DOMAIN` with CHECK constraint (email_address, positive_int, slug)
- [x] **NEW** — `CREATE DOMAIN ... NOT NULL` (slug domain)
- [x] EXISTING — `CREATE TYPE ... AS ENUM` (Pagila has mpaa_rating; here: document_status, user_role)

Note: Pagila has two domains (`year`, `bıgınt`) but they are trivial aliases. The synthetic DB adds domains with regex CHECK constraints and NOT NULL.

### Index Types

- [x] **NEW** — GIN indexes on jsonb columns (idx_users_settings_gin, idx_documents_metadata_gin, etc.)
- [x] **NEW** — GIN indexes on text[] array columns (idx_users_tags_gin, idx_documents_tags_gin)
- [x] **NEW** — GiST indexes on tsvector columns (idx_users_search_gist, idx_documents_search_gist)
- [x] **NEW** — Partial indexes with WHERE clause (idx_documents_active, idx_documents_draft, idx_users_active, idx_audit_recent)
- [x] **NEW** — Expression indexes (idx_users_email_lower, idx_documents_title_lower, idx_categories_slug_lower)
- [x] EXISTING — Standard B-tree indexes (all test DBs have these)

Note: Pagila has one GiST index (film_fulltext_idx) but it is the only one across all test DBs. The synthetic DB adds multiple GIN and GiST indexes with different column types.

### Row Level Security

- [x] **NEW** — `ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`
- [x] **NEW** — `CREATE POLICY ... FOR SELECT USING (...)`
- [x] **NEW** — `CREATE POLICY ... FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (...)`
- [x] **NEW** — `CREATE POLICY ... FOR UPDATE USING (...)`

### EXCLUDE Constraints

- [x] **NEW** — `EXCLUDE USING gist (room_name WITH =, booked_during WITH &&)` on booking_slots

### COMMENT ON

- [x] **NEW** — `COMMENT ON TABLE` (8 tables documented)
- [x] **NEW** — `COMMENT ON COLUMN` (9 columns documented)
- [x] **NEW** — `COMMENT ON INDEX` (4 indexes documented)

Note: None of the existing test DBs (Pagila, Chinook, Northwind) contain COMMENT ON statements in their DDL.

### Other Standard Features (present for completeness)

- [x] EXISTING — Primary keys (PK)
- [x] EXISTING — Foreign keys (FK) with ON DELETE CASCADE / SET NULL / RESTRICT
- [x] EXISTING — CHECK constraints
- [x] EXISTING — UNIQUE constraints (single and composite)
- [x] EXISTING — NOT NULL
- [x] EXISTING — DEFAULT values (constants, now(), enum literals)
- [x] **NEW** — `GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY` (PG10+)
- [x] **NEW** — `GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY` (PG10+)
- [x] EXISTING — Self-referencing FK (categories.parent_id)
- [x] EXISTING — Composite primary key (document_tags)
- [x] **NEW** — inet column type (audit_log.ip_address)
- [x] **NEW** — tstzrange column type (booking_slots.booked_during)
- [x] EXISTING — tsvector column type (Pagila has film.fulltext)
- [x] EXISTING — jsonb columns
- [x] EXISTING — text[] array columns
- [x] **NEW** — Extensions: btree_gist
- [x] EXISTING — Trigger functions (plpgsql)
- [x] EXISTING — Triggers (BEFORE/AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE)

## SQL Converter Results

The converter (`go run ./cmd/pgdesigner convert`) **successfully parsed** the entire `schema.sql` file with no errors. Output: `schema.pgd` (292 lines of valid XML).

### What was captured correctly

| Feature | Status |
|---|---|
| Domains (email_address, positive_int, slug) with constraints | OK |
| Enums (document_status, user_role) | OK |
| All 8 tables with columns, types, defaults | OK |
| Identity columns (ALWAYS, BY DEFAULT) | OK |
| Foreign keys with actions | OK |
| CHECK constraints | OK |
| UNIQUE constraints | OK |
| GIN indexes | OK |
| GiST indexes | OK |
| Partial indexes (WHERE clause) | OK |
| Expression indexes | OK |
| COMMENT ON TABLE/COLUMN/INDEX | OK |
| Extension (btree_gist) | OK |
| Functions (3 plpgsql trigger functions) | OK |
| Triggers (4 triggers) | OK |
| Explicit named PK (pk_document_tags) | OK |

### What was silently dropped (converter limitations)

These statements parsed without error but produced no output in the .pgd file:

| Feature | SQL Statement | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| **Inline PRIMARY KEY on column** | `id bigint GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY` | `convertColumnDef()` handles NOTNULL, DEFAULT, IDENTITY but not CONSTR_PRIMARY. Affects 7 of 8 tables (all except document_tags which uses explicit table-level PK). |
| **EXCLUDE constraint** | `CONSTRAINT excl_booking_no_overlap EXCLUDE USING gist (...)` | `convertTableConstraint()` has no case for `CONSTR_EXCLUSION`. The constraint is silently dropped. |
| **Row Level Security** | `ALTER TABLE documents ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` | `convertAlterTable()` only handles AT_AddConstraint, AT_AttachPartition, and AT_AddIdentity subtypes. RLS enable is a different subtype. |
| **CREATE POLICY** | `CREATE POLICY documents_select_policy ON documents ...` | `convertStmt()` has no case for `Node_CreatePolicyStmt`. The pgd XML has an empty `<policies>` section. |

### Recommendations for Go converter (do NOT modify now)

1. **Inline PK on columns** — Add `CONSTR_PRIMARY` handling in `convertColumnDef()` or in `convertTableConstraint()` (when the constraint comes from column-level definition). This is the highest-priority fix since it affects most real-world schemas.
2. **EXCLUDE constraints** — Add a `CONSTR_EXCLUSION` case in `convertTableConstraint()`. The pgd model already supports `<exclude>` elements.
3. **RLS enable** — Handle `AT_EnableRowLevelSecurity` in `convertAlterTable()`.
4. **CREATE POLICY** — Add a `Node_CreatePolicyStmt` case in `convertStmt()`. The pgd model already has a `<policies>` section.
